title: No Deposit Houses for Rent in Cleveland
metaTitle: No Deposit Houses for Rent in Cleveland, OH
metaDescription: Searching for no deposit houses for rent in Cleveland? Here's the honest picture on deposits, Ohio law, and what actually helps. Book a free showing.
primaryKeyword: no deposit houses for rent cleveland
answerBox: True $0-deposit houses are rare in Cleveland's private rental market. Rent Finder Cleveland, like most local landlords, generally requires a security deposit — commonly around one month's rent — to cover potential damages, per standard Ohio landlord practice. A Section 8 voucher covers monthly rent, not the deposit. Ask about deposit specifics and any flexibility when you book a showing.

sections.h2: Are there truly no-deposit houses for rent in Cleveland?
body: Genuinely deposit-free house rentals are uncommon in Cleveland's private rental market. Most landlords, Rent Finder Cleveland included, require a **security deposit** — typically in the range of one month's rent — before move-in, since it's the standard way to cover potential unpaid rent or damage beyond normal wear and tear. We manage 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, and while deposit amounts vary by unit and applicant, we don't advertise or promise deposit-free move-ins.

If deposit cost is the real obstacle, it's worth asking directly rather than searching for a "no deposit" listing that may not exist: some landlords will consider a reduced deposit, an installment arrangement, or other terms based on rental history and application strength. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and raise it with our leasing team before you apply.

sections.h2: What does Ohio law say about security deposits?
body: Ohio law generally treats a security deposit as refundable, with specific rules on how it must be handled. Under [Ohio Revised Code §5321.16](https://codes.ohio.gov/ohio-revised-code/section-5321.16), a deposit exceeding $50 or one month's rent (whichever is greater) must earn 5% annual interest on the excess if the tenant stays six months or more, and the landlord must return the deposit — with an itemized written statement of any deductions — within 30 days of the tenant moving out and providing a forwarding address. A landlord who wrongfully withholds a deposit can be liable for the withheld amount plus an equal amount in damages, and the tenant's reasonable attorney's fees.

This is informational only, not legal advice — verify current statute and your specific lease terms. Our guides on [Ohio security deposit law](/cleveland-rentals/guides/ohio-security-deposit-law/) and [getting your deposit back in Ohio](/cleveland-rentals/guides/get-security-deposit-back-ohio/) go into more detail on the return and itemization process.

sections.h2: What actually helps if a full deposit is a stretch?
body: Rather than chasing a "no deposit" listing, a few things genuinely move the needle when a deposit is tight: strong rental references and a clean payment history, a co-signer or guarantor who can back the lease, proof of steady income that clearly covers rent, and being upfront with the landlord about the situation before applying rather than after. [Our guide to renting with a cosigner](/cleveland-rentals/guides/cosigner-to-rent-apartment-ohio/) walks through how that works in Ohio, and [renters insurance](/cleveland-rentals/guides/renters-insurance-cleveland-cost/) — while it doesn't replace a deposit — is a low-cost way to show a landlord you're planning ahead.

rows: Commonly ~1 month's rent; governed by ORC 5321.16
Backs the lease if you fall short on income/credit
Doesn't eliminate the deposit, but can help approval
Ask your current/former landlord for a reference letter
Some flexibility varies by unit and applicant
Raise it before applying, not after

sections.h2: Do Section 8 vouchers cover the deposit?
body: No — a Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) subsidizes a portion of the **monthly rent**, not the upfront security deposit. Voucher holders should budget for the deposit separately, the same as any other renter. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland manages accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready, so the voucher process itself doesn't add extra deposit requirements beyond what any applicant would pay. See our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the full application and inspection process through CMHA.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent a house in Cleveland overall?
body: Deposit size is usually tied to monthly rent, so knowing typical rent ranges helps with budgeting. Houses in our Cleveland portfolio generally run $750–$1,100 a month for two bedrooms and $1,000–$1,500 for three bedrooms. Citywide, Zumper's rent report dated July 4, 2026 put the median asking rent at $1,250/mo, while RentCafe's report dated July 2, 2026 put the professionally-managed average at $1,564/mo. Budgeting for both first month's rent and a comparable deposit up front is the realistic starting point for most Cleveland house rentals.

faq.q: Can I really find a house for rent in Cleveland with no security deposit?
faq.a: It's uncommon. Most landlords, including us, require a standard security deposit to cover potential damage or unpaid rent, generally around one month's rent. If cost is the concern, ask directly about flexibility rather than searching for a listing that may not exist.

faq.q: How much is a typical security deposit in Cleveland?
faq.a: There's no fixed citywide amount, but one month's rent is a common benchmark. Ohio law (ORC 5321.16) requires deposits over $50 or one month's rent to earn 5% annual interest on the excess if you stay six months or longer, and requires the deposit back within 30 days of move-out with an itemized statement.

faq.q: Does a Section 8 voucher cover the security deposit?
faq.a: No. A voucher subsidizes monthly rent, not the upfront deposit. Voucher holders should budget for the deposit the same as any other applicant. All homes we manage accept Section 8 and are HUD-inspection-ready.

faq.q: What can I do if I can't afford the full deposit?
faq.a: Ask the landlord directly — some will consider a reduced deposit, installment plan, or added flexibility based on your rental history, income, or a co-signer. Strong references and being upfront before applying help more than searching for a deposit-free listing.

faq.q: When do I get my security deposit back in Ohio?
faq.a: Ohio law generally requires landlords to return the deposit, with an itemized statement of any deductions, within 30 days after the lease ends and you provide a forwarding address. This is informational only — verify current statute and your lease terms.
